docs.title: Persona Guide
docs.text: This guide explains FlowPilot's persona system, including the data model, API operations, authorization rules, and technical implementation details.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
A persona represents a business role that a user assumes in a specific context. Personas are the foundation of FlowPilot's authorization model and drive policy decisions for both autonomous AI booking and delegated access.

Key Concepts:

  • A single user may have multiple personas (e.g., both traveler and travel-agent)
  • Each authorization request evaluates against exactly one active persona
  • Personas include autobook preferences (consent, cost limits, lead time, risk tolerance)
  • Personas have temporal validity (valid_from, valid_till) and status (active, inactive, suspended)
  • Personas are user-owned and independently configurable

docs.title: Core Attributes
docs.text: Field Type Description persona_id UUID Unique identifier for the persona user_sub String Owner's user ID (Firebase UID or Keycloak sub) title String Persona title (e.g., "traveler", "travel-agent") status String Current status: "active", "inactive", "suspended", "expired" scope Array[String] Allowed actions (e.g., ["read", "execute"]) valid_from ISO 8601 Timestamp when persona becomes active valid_till ISO 8601 Timestamp when persona expires created_at ISO 8601 Creation timestamp updated_at ISO 8601 Last modification timestamp

docs.title: Autobook Attributes
docs.text:
Autobook attributes define autonomous AI booking preferences:

Field Type Description autobook_consent Boolean Whether user allows autonomous AI booking autobook_price Integer Maximum booking price (EUR) autobook_leadtime Integer Minimum advance booking time (days) autobook_risklevel Integer Maximum airline risk score (0-100)

docs.title: Example Persona Document
docs.text:
{
  "persona_id": "b9678f30-f4b0-4033-82db-846357311165",
  "user_sub": "PKbHpCqDnLcNywEo8pev8yQmoU43",
  "title": "traveler",
  "status": "active",
  "scope": ["read", "execute"],
  "valid_from": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "valid_till": "2026-12-31T23:59:59Z",
  "created_at": "2026-01-13T17:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-01-13T17:00:00Z",
  "autobook_consent": true,
  "autobook_price": 10000,
  "autobook_leadtime": 7,
  "autobook_risklevel": 5
}

docs.title: Supported Persona Titles
docs.text: FlowPilot defines the following persona titles for the "Travel" use case:

docs.title: End-User Personas
docs.text:
• traveler - End users who book travel for themselves
    • Can own workflows
    • Can delegate to agents
    • Autobook preferences apply to their workflows

docs.title: Delegation Personas
docs.text:
• travel-agent - Professional travel agents

    • Can execute workflows on behalf of travelers (with delegation)
    • Trusted role for booking operations

  • office-manager - Office administrators

    • Can manage travel bookings for employees
    • Can execute workflows with delegation

  • booking-assistant - Booking support staff

    • Can assist with travel bookings
    • Can execute workflows with delegation

docs.title: System Personas
docs.text:
• ai-agent - Autonomous AI agents
    • System-only persona (not stored in user-profile-api)
    • Used by ai-agent-api and domain-services-api
    • Subject to autobook policy gates

docs.title: 1. Creation
docs.text:
Personas are created via the user-profile-api POST /v1/personas endpoint.

Request: 

POST /v1/personas
Authorization: Bearer <user-token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "title": "traveler",
  "scope": ["read", "execute"],
  "valid_from": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "valid_till": "2026-12-31T23:59:59Z",
  "status": "active",
  "autobook_consent": true,
  "autobook_price": 5000,
  "autobook_leadtime": 7,
  "autobook_risklevel": 3
}

Response: 

{
  "persona_id": "b9678f30-f4b0-4033-82db-846357311165",
  "user_sub": "PKbHpCqDnLcNywEo8pev8yQmoU43",
  "title": "traveler",
  "status": "active",
  "scope": ["read", "execute"],
  "valid_from": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "valid_till": "2026-12-31T23:59:59Z",
  "created_at": "2026-01-13T17:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-01-13T17:00:00Z",
  "autobook_consent": true,
  "autobook_price": 5000,
  "autobook_leadtime": 7,
  "autobook_risklevel": 3
}

Authorization: The user must be authenticated. The persona is created for the authenticated user (extracted from JWT sub claim).

docs.title: 2. Listing
docs.text:
Users can list their own personas:

GET /v1/personas?status=active
Authorization: Bearer <user-token>

Service accounts can list personas for any user:

GET /v1/users/{user_sub}/personas?status=active
Authorization: Bearer <service-token>

This endpoint is used by authz-api to fetch persona data for authorization decisions.

docs.title: 3. Retrieval
docs.text:
Fetch a specific persona by ID:

GET /v1/personas/{persona_id}
Authorization: Bearer <user-token>

Authorization: The persona must belong to the authenticated user.

docs.title: 4. Update
docs.text:
Modify persona attributes:

PUT /v1/personas/{persona_id}
Authorization: Bearer <user-token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "autobook_price": 8000,
  "autobook_leadtime": 14,
  "status": "active"
}

All fields are optional (partial update). Only provided fields are updated.

Authorization: The persona must belong to the authenticated user.

docs.title: 5. Deletion
docs.text:
Delete a persona:

DELETE /v1/personas/{persona_id}
Authorization: Bearer <user-token>

Authorization: The persona must belong to the authenticated user.

docs.title: Storage Backends
docs.text: FlowPilot supports two storage backends for personas:

docs.title: Firestore (GCP Production)
docs.text:
Used by Cloud Run deployment with Firebase Authentication: - Serverless, scalable NoSQL database - Collection: personas - Document ID = persona_id - Requires composite indexes for efficient queries

Required Firestore Indexes:

# Index 1: user_sub + status + created_at (descending)
gcloud firestore indexes composite create \
  --collection-group=personas \
  --query-scope=COLLECTION \
  --field-config field-path=user_sub,order=ascending \
  --field-config field-path=status,order=ascending \
  --field-config field-path=created_at,order=descending

# Index 2: user_sub + created_at (descending)
gcloud firestore indexes composite create \
  --collection-group=personas \
  --query-scope=COLLECTION \
  --field-config field-path=user_sub,order=ascending \
  --field-config field-path=created_at,order=descending

docs.title: SQLite (Local Development)
docs.text: Used by local Docker Compose stack with Keycloak: - Serverless - In-memory or file-based SQLite database - Schema defined in personadb_sqlite.py - Simple, zero-configuration setup

docs.title: User-Profile-API
docs.text:
Purpose: Persona lifecycle management (CRUD operations)

Endpoints:

  • POST /v1/personas - Create persona
  • GET /v1/personas - List user's personas
  • GET /v1/personas/{persona_id} - Get specific persona
  • PUT /v1/personas/{persona_id} - Update persona
  • DELETE /v1/personas/{persona_id} - Delete persona
  • GET /v1/users/{user_sub}/personas - List personas for any user (service accounts only)

Authorization:

  • User endpoints: JWT sub must match persona owner
  • Service endpoint: Requires service account token (Keycloak: client_id=flowpilot-agent, GCP: gserviceaccount.com in email)

Code Location: flowpilot-services/user-profile-api/

docs.title: AuthZ-API
docs.text:
Purpose: Fetches persona data for authorization decisions (acts as Policy Information Point)

Persona Fetching Logic:

  1. Extract owner.id and owner.persona (title) from AuthZEN request
  2. If owner.persona_id is present, fetch directly: GET /v1/personas/{persona_id}
  3. If only owner.persona (title) is present, fetch by user and title: GET /v1/users/{user_sub}/personas
  4. If fetch fails (404, 403, timeout), use default values (deny by default)
  5. Augment resource.properties.owner with autobook attributes for OPA

Code Location: flowpilot-services/authz-api/authz_core.py

Key Functions:

  • fetch_persona_from_api(persona_id) - Fetch by ID
  • fetch_persona_by_user_and_title(user_sub, persona_title) - Fetch by user and title
  • build_opa_input() - Augment OPA input with persona data

docs.title: OPA Integration
docs.text:
Persona data flows into OPA as part of the resource.properties.owner object:

{
  "subject": {"id": "agent-runner", "persona": "ai-agent"},
  "action": {"name": "execute"},
  "resource": {
    "properties": {
      "owner": {
        "id": "user-uuid",
        "persona": "traveler",
        "persona_id": "b9678f30-f4b0-4033-82db-846357311165",
        "persona_status": "active",
        "persona_valid_from": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
        "persona_valid_till": "2026-12-31T23:59:59Z",
        "autobook_consent": true,
        "autobook_price": 5000,
        "autobook_leadtime": 7,
        "autobook_risklevel": 3
      }
    }
  }
}

OPA policies use these attributes to evaluate authorization gates:

# Consent check
has_consent if {
  input.resource.properties.owner.autobook_consent == true
}

# Cost gate
within_cost_limit if {
  planned_price := input.resource.properties.planned_price
  max_cost := input.resource.properties.owner.autobook_price
  planned_price <= max_cost
}

# Persona status check
owner_persona_active if {
  input.resource.properties.owner.persona_status == "active"
}

# Persona temporal validity
owner_persona_valid_time if {
  valid_from_str := input.resource.properties.owner.persona_valid_from
  valid_till_str := input.resource.properties.owner.persona_valid_till
  valid_from := time.parse_rfc3339_ns(valid_from_str)
  valid_till := time.parse_rfc3339_ns(valid_till_str)
  now := time.now_ns()
  now >= valid_from
  now <= valid_till
}

docs.title: OPA Policy Rules
docs.text: The auto_book.rego policy enforces these persona rules:

docs.title: Execute Action Rules
docs.text:
1. Authorized Principal + Persona Validation

    • Owner executing their own workflow: subject.persona must match owner.persona
    • Delegated user: subject.persona must be one of: travel-agent, office-manager, booking-assistant, ai-agent
    • AI agent activated by owner: context.principal.persona must match owner.persona
    • AI agent activated by delegate: context.principal.persona must be an agent persona

  2. Persona Status

    • owner.persona_status must be "active"
    • If missing, defaults to allowing (backward compatibility)

  3. Persona Temporal Validity

    • Current time must be between owner.persona_valid_from and owner.persona_valid_till
    • If missing, defaults to allowing (backward compatibility)

docs.title: Read Action Rules
docs.text:
1. Owner can always read (no persona restrictions)
  2. AI agents can read (needed to list workflow items)
  3. Delegated users with read permission:
    • Must have valid delegation with "read" action
    • subject.persona must be either:
    • An agent persona (travel-agent, office-manager, booking-assistant, ai-agent)
    • OR match owner.persona

docs.title: API Validation Rules
docs.text:
The user-profile-api enforces these constraints:

  • title must be one of the allowed persona titles (configurable via ALLOWED_PERSONA_TITLES env var)
  • scope must be a non-empty array of action strings
  • valid_from & valid_till must be valid ISO 8601 timestamps
  • status must be one of: "active", "inactive", "suspended", "expired"
  • autobook_price must be a non-negative integer
  • autobook_leadtime must be a non-negative integer
  • autobook_risklevel must be an integer between 0 and 100

docs.title: Local Development (Keycloak)
docs.text:
Use the seed_keycloak_users.py script:

python3 flowpilot-provisioning/seed_keycloak_users.py \
  --csv flowpilot-provisioning/users_seed.csv

docs.title: GCP Production (Firebase)
docs.text:
Use the seed_firebase_users.py script:

FIREBASE_API_KEY=<your-api-key> \
python3 flowpilot-provisioning/seed_firebase_users.py \
  --profile-api-url=https://flowpilot-user-profile-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app \
  --csv=flowpilot-provisioning/users_seed.csv

docs.title: CSV Format
docs.text:
The users_seed.csv file defines users and their personas:

username;password;email;firstname;lastname;persona;autobook_consent;autobook_price;autobook_leadtime;autobook_risklevel;persona_status;persona_valid_from;persona_valid_till
carlo;password;carlo@me.com;Carlo;Schupp;traveler;Yes;10000;7;5;active;2024-01-01T00:00:00Z;2026-12-31T23:59:59Z
yannick;password;yannick@me.com;Yannick;De Backer;travel-agent,office-manager;Yes;1500;7;2;active;2024-01-01T00:00:00Z;2026-12-31T23:59:59Z

Note: Users can have multiple personas by comma-separating them in the persona column. Each persona is created independently with the same autobook settings.

docs.title: Scenario 1: Owner with Traveler Persona
docs.text:
Context:

  • Carlo (traveler) creates a workflow
  • Carlo executes his own workflow

Authorization Flow:

  1. Domain-services-api receives request with Carlo's token
  2. Authz-api extracts sub=carlo-uuid from JWT
  3. Authz-api fetches Carlo's "traveler" persona from user-profile-api
  4. OPA evaluates:
    • authorized_principal: ✓ (owner == principal)
    • persona_valid: ✓ (traveler == traveler)
    • owner_persona_active: ✓ (status == "active")
    • owner_persona_valid_time: ✓ (current time within range)
    • has_consent: ✓ (autobook_consent == true)
    • Other gates...
  5. Decision: Allow (if all gates pass)

docs.title: Scenario 2: Delegated Travel Agent
docs.text:
Context:

  • Carlo (traveler) delegates to Yannick (travel-agent)
  • Yannick executes Carlo's workflow

Authorization Flow:

  1. Domain-services-api receives request with Yannick's token
  2. Authz-api extracts sub=yannick-uuid from JWT
  3. Authz-api queries delegation-api: valid delegation exists
  4. Authz-api fetches Carlo's "traveler" persona (resource owner)
  5. OPA evaluates:
    • authorized_principal: ✓ (valid delegation with "execute" action)
    • persona_valid: ✓ (Yannick's persona "travel-agent" is an agent persona)
    • owner_persona_active: ✓ (Carlo's persona status == "active")
    • owner_persona_valid_time: ✓ (Carlo's persona is valid)
    • has_consent: ✓ (Carlo's autobook_consent == true)
    • Other gates...
  6. Decision: Allow (if all gates pass)

docs.title: Scenario 3: Autonomous AI Agent
docs.text:
Context:

  • AI agent attempts to book autonomously (no delegation)
  • Carlo's workflow with autobook consent

Authorization Flow:

  1. AI-agent-api calls domain-services-api with service token
  2. AuthZEN request: subject.persona = "ai-agent", context.principal.id = carlo-uuid
  3. Authz-api fetches Carlo's "traveler" persona
  4. OPA evaluates:
    • authorized_principal: ✓ (autobook_consent == true, no delegation required)
    • persona_valid: ✓ (context.principal.persona == owner.persona)
    • owner_persona_active: ✓ (status == "active")
    • owner_persona_valid_time: ✓ (within valid time range)
    • has_consent: ✓ (autobook_consent == true)
    • within_cost_limit: Check Carlo's autobook_price
    • sufficient_advance: Check Carlo's autobook_leadtime
    • acceptable_risk: Check Carlo's autobook_risklevel
  5. Decision: Allow or Deny based on ABAC gates

docs.title: Scenario 4: Persona Mismatch
docs.text:
Context:

  • Martine has two personas: "traveler" and "office-manager"
  • Martine (office-manager) tries to execute her own workflow created with "traveler" persona

Authorization Flow:

  1. Workflow was created with owner.persona = "traveler"
  2. Martine's request has subject.persona = "office-manager"
  3. OPA evaluates:
    • authorized_principal: ✓ (owner == principal)
    • persona_valid: ✗ (office-manager ≠ traveler, and office-manager is not allowed for owner execution)
  4. Decision: Deny with reason_code "auto_book.persona_mismatch"

Resolution: Martine must switch to her "traveler" persona when executing her own workflows.

docs.title: Persona Not Found
docs.text:
Symptom: Authorization denied with auto_book.no_consent despite user having consent

Causes:

  1. Persona not created or provisioned
  2. Persona fetch fails (403, 404, timeout)
  3. Service account cannot access user-profile-api

Debug Steps:

  1. Verify persona exists: 

    curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <user-token>" \
  https://user-profile-api/v1/personas

  2. Check authz-api logs for persona fetch errors: 

    gcloud logging read "resource.labels.service_name=flowpilot-authz-api \
  AND (textPayload=~'persona' OR jsonPayload.error=~'persona')" \
  --limit=20

  3. Verify service account can access user-profile-api: 

    # Get service token (GCP)
curl -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/identity?audience=flowpilot-services"

# Test access
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <service-token>" \
  https://user-profile-api/v1/users/<user-sub>/personas

docs.title: Persona Status Denied
docs.text:
Symptom: Authorization denied with auto_book.persona_inactive or auto_book.persona_expired

Causes:

  1. Persona status is not "active"
  2. Current time is outside valid_from/valid_till range

Resolution:

  1. Update persona status: 

    curl -X PUT https://user-profile-api/v1/personas/<persona-id> \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <user-token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"status": "active"}'

  2. Update temporal validity: 

    curl -X PUT https://user-profile-api/v1/personas/<persona-id> \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <user-token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "valid_from": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
    "valid_till": "2027-12-31T23:59:59Z"
  }'

docs.title: Firestore Index Missing
docs.text:
Symptom: User-profile-api returns 500 error: "The query requires an index"

Resolution: Create the required composite indexes (see "Storage Backends" section above)

docs.title: Service Account Authorization Failure
docs.text:
Symptom: User-profile-api returns 403: "Forbidden: Service account required"

Causes:

  1. Service token doesn't have client_id=flowpilot-agent (Keycloak)
  2. Service token doesn't have gserviceaccount.com in email (GCP)

Resolution:

  • Local: Use correct Keycloak service account credentials
  • GCP: Ensure authz-api uses GCP identity tokens from metadata server

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use descriptive persona titles - Match business roles, not technical identities
  2. Set reasonable autobook limits - Start conservative, adjust based on user comfort
  3. Validate temporal ranges - Ensure valid_till is far enough in the future
  4. Monitor persona status - Implement workflows to expire/suspend personas when needed
  5. Limit personas per user - Most users should have 1-2 personas (configurable via MAX_PERSONAS_PER_USER)
  6. Test delegation with personas - Verify agent personas work correctly with delegations
  7. Use active personas for authorization - Inactive/suspended personas should not be used
  8. Provision test data properly - Use seed scripts to maintain consistency across environments

docs.text:
• Policy Development Guide - How OPA policies use persona data
  • Authorization Architecture - Overall authorization flow
  • API Reference: User Profile API - Full API specification
  • Delegation Guide - How personas interact with delegations

docs.title: Policy Development Guide
docs.text:
This guide explains how FlowPilot’s authorization policies are structured, how they are evaluated, and the role of the authz-api in the overall authorization flow.

FlowPilot uses Open Policy Agent (OPA) with Rego policies to implement declarative access control. At its core, the policy engine answers the question:

“Is this subject allowed to perform this action on this resource?”

docs.title: Policy Decision Flow
docs.text: FlowPilot deliberately separates authorization responsibilities across three layers.

docs.title: Policy Decision Layer (PDP)
docs.text:
• Is implemented using OPA
  • Is driven by declarative rules written in Rego
  • Is limited to pure policy logic
  • Performs no data fetching or external calls
  • Receives fully prepared, decision-ready input via AuthZEN

docs.title: Policy Information Layer (Authz-API)
docs.text:
• Acts as the authorization orchestration layer
  • Consolidates data from multiple Policy Information Points (PIPs)
  • Validates and interprets JWT bearer tokens
  • Fetches user profile data (ABAC attributes)
  • Resolves delegation chains (ReBAC attributes)
  • Normalizes, enriches, and hardens all inputs
  • Invokes OPA with a complete and consistent policy context

docs.title: Policy Enforcement Layer (PEP)
docs.text:
• Submits AuthZEN requests that express intent
  • Applies and enforces decisions returned by the PDP
  • Never evaluates authorization logic locally

This separation ensures that:

  • Policies remain clean, declarative, and testable
  • Authorization logic is centralized and auditable
  • Application services stay simple and focused on business behavior

docs.title: Notes on OPA and Rego
docs.text:
OPA is a CNCF Graduated project (graduated January 29, 2021), which signals maturity, governance, and production readiness. However, it is not a formal international standard.

  • OPA is open source and published under the Apache License 2.0
  • Rego, the policy language used by OPA, is likewise not a formal standard
  • The authoritative definition of Rego semantics and behavior is provided by the OPA project itself:
  • The OPA documentation serves as the canonical language reference
  • Built-in functions are defined, versioned, and maintained by OPA

In practice, this means Rego policies are portable across OPA deployments, while remaining tightly aligned with the OPA project’s evolution.

docs.title: AuthZEN-based orchestration
docs.text:
The authz-api acts as the orchestration layer between PEPs and the PDP. It receives AuthZEN packages as input from PEPs and produces AuthZEN packages for the PDP. To achieve this, the authz-api:

  1. Validates JWTs - Ensures bearer tokens are valid
  2. Resolves Delegation - Queries delegation-api for ReBAC data
  3. Enriches Context - Adds policy-relevant attributes (consent, thresholds)
  4. Normalizes Data - Converts types (dates to RFC3339, strings to numbers)
  5. Calls OPA - Sends enriched input to policy engine
  6. Returns Decision - Structured allow/deny with reason codes

Key Insight: While the authz-api receives intent from PEPs and ensures the PDP receives decision-ready facts, not raw requests.

docs.title: Policy Construction
docs.text:
FlowPilot's primary policy is auto_book.rego, which controls autonomous AI booking and delegated access.

Policy is stored in a file of type REGO at: 

infra/opa/policies/auto_book.rego

docs.title: Step 0. Supported Actions
docs.text:
• execute - Execute workflow items (book travel)
  • read - View workflows (read-only access)

docs.title: Step 1: Default Deny
docs.text:
default allow := false

What this does:

  • Fail-closed by default - Deny unless explicitly allowed

Authz-API role: Interprets allow = false as a deny decision

docs.title: Step 2: Decision Routing
docs.text:
allow if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  allow_execute
}

allow if {
  input.action.name == "read"
  allow_read
}

What this does:

  • Routes to different rule sets based on the requested action
  • execute requires full authorization gates
  • read has simpler requirements

Authz-API role: Includes action.name in the OPA input based on the PEP's request

docs.title: Step 3: Decisions Gates
docs.text:
allow_execute if {
  authorized_principal  # Anti-spoofing and delegation check
  persona_valid         # Persona check
  has_consent          # User consent
  acceptable_risk      # Risk threshold
  within_cost_limit    # Cost ceiling
  sufficient_advance   # Lead time requirement
}

What this does:

  • Defines 6 gates that all must pass for execution
  • Each gate is a separate rule evaluated independently
  • If any gate fails, allow_execute is false

Authz-API role: Provides all data needed for gate evaluation (more below)

docs.title: Step 4: Anti-Spoofing Checks
docs.text:
# Owner directly accessing their own resource
authorized_principal if {
  input.subject.persona != "ai-agent"
  principal_id := input.subject.id
  owner_id := input.resource.properties.owner.id
  principal_id == owner_id
}

# AI-agent activated by the owner
authorized_principal if {
  input.subject.persona == "ai-agent"
  principal_id := input.context.principal.id
  owner_id := input.resource.properties.owner.id
  principal_id == owner_id
}

# AI-agent in autonomous mode
authorized_principal if {
  input.subject.persona == "ai-agent"
  input.resource.properties.owner.autobook_consent == true
  not input.context.delegation.valid
}

# Valid delegation
authorized_principal if {
  has_valid_delegation_for_action
}

What this does:

  • Prevents principal spoofing (acting as someone else without permission)
  • Allows 4 scenarios:
  • Owner acting directly - User executes their own workflow
  • Owner via agent - User activates AI agent to execute
  • Autonomous AI - AI acts independently with consent
  • Delegated user - Another user with explicit delegation

Authz-API role:

  • Queries delegation-api to get context.delegation.valid
  • Includes context.delegation.delegated_actions array
  • Ensures context.principal is set when subject ≠ owner

Delegation Check Helper:

has_valid_delegation_for_action if {
  input.context.delegation.valid == true
  input.action.name in input.context.delegation.delegated_actions
}

This checks if: - Delegation exists (valid == true) - Delegation grants the requested action (e.g., execute)

docs.title: Step 5: Persona Validation
docs.text:
valid_agent_personas := {"travel-agent", "ai-agent", "office-manager", "booking-assistant"}

# Owner executing directly
persona_valid if {
  input.subject.persona != "ai-agent"
  principal_id := input.subject.id
  owner_id := input.resource.properties.owner.id
  principal_id == owner_id
  owner_persona := input.resource.properties.owner.persona
  input.subject.persona == owner_persona
}

# Owner executing via AI-agent
persona_valid if {
  input.subject.persona == "ai-agent"
  principal_id := input.context.principal.id
  owner_id := input.resource.properties.owner.id
  principal_id == owner_id
  owner_persona := input.resource.properties.owner.persona
  principal_persona := input.context.principal.persona
  principal_persona == owner_persona
}

# Human user delegated by owner, or other delegated user (in a chain of delegation)
persona_valid if {
  input.subject.persona != "ai-agent"
  principal_id := input.subject.id
  owner_id := input.resource.properties.owner.id
  principal_id != owner_id
  input.subject.persona != ""
  valid_agent_personas[input.subject.persona]
}

# AI-agent activated by delegated user
persona_valid if {
  input.subject.persona == "ai-agent"
  principal_id := input.context.principal.id
  owner_id := input.resource.properties.owner.id
  principal_id != owner_id
  principal_persona := input.context.principal.persona
  principal_persona != ""
  valid_agent_personas[principal_persona]
}

What this does:

  • Validates that the user's business role (persona) is appropriate
  • Delegated users must have agent personas (travel-agent, etc.)
  • Owners must match their own persona

Authz-API role:

  • Extracts persona from JWT custom claims
  • Fetches complete persona object from user-profile-api (includes autobook attributes, status, validity)
  • Includes both subject.persona and enriched owner object in OPA input

Important: The owner.persona field contains the persona title (e.g., "traveler"), while the full persona data (with autobook attributes, status, and temporal validity) is fetched from user-profile-api and merged into resource.properties.owner.

docs.title: Step 5a: Persona Status and Temporal Validity
docs.text: In addition to persona matching, the policy enforces two additional persona-level gates:

docs.title: Persona Status Check
docs.text:
owner_persona_active if {
  # If no persona_status field, assume active (backward compatibility)
  not input.resource.properties.owner.persona_status
}

owner_persona_active if {
  # Persona status must be "active"
  input.resource.properties.owner.persona_status == "active"
}

Ensures the owner's persona is in "active" status. Personas can be:

  • active - Normal operational state
  • inactive - Temporarily disabled
  • suspended - Administratively suspended
  • expired - Past validity period

docs.title: Persona Temporal Validity Check
docs.text:
owner_persona_valid_time if {
  # If no temporal fields, assume valid
  not input.resource.properties.owner.persona_valid_from
  not input.resource.properties.owner.persona_valid_till
}

owner_persona_valid_time if {
  # Parse and check timestamps
  valid_from_str := input.resource.properties.owner.persona_valid_from
  valid_till_str := input.resource.properties.owner.persona_valid_till
  valid_from_str != ""
  valid_till_str != ""

  valid_from := time.parse_rfc3339_ns(valid_from_str)
  valid_till := time.parse_rfc3339_ns(valid_till_str)
  now := time.now_ns()

  now >= valid_from
  now <= valid_till
}

Checks if current time is within the persona's validity period (valid_from to valid_till).

Use cases:

  • Time-limited personas (temporary roles, seasonal access)
  • Trial periods with automatic expiration
  • Future-dated persona activation

Authz-API role for persona attributes:

  • Queries user-profile-api: GET /v1/users/{user_sub}/personas?status=active
  • Finds persona matching owner.persona title
  • Extracts: persona_status, valid_from, valid_till, and all autobook_* fields
  • Merges into resource.properties.owner for OPA evaluation

docs.title: Step 6: Consent Check
docs.text:
has_consent if {
  input.resource.properties.owner.autobook_consent == true
}

What this does:

  • Requires explicit user opt-in for autonomous booking
  • Consent is per-user, not per-workflow

Authz-API role:

  • Fetches persona from user-profile-api
  • Extracts autobook_consent boolean from persona record
  • Includes in resource.properties.owner.autobook_consent

Important: Autobook consent is per-persona, not per-user. A user with multiple personas can have different consent settings for each.

docs.title: Step 7: User-declared Constraints Check
docs.text:
A user can set constraits to the AI-agent about when it can act autonomously.

In the use case of "travel", this reflects the risk a traveler is willing to take in terms of cost, transport risk, and time to change their mind.

within_cost_limit if {
  planned_price := input.resource.properties.planned_price
  planned_price <= input.resource.properties.owner.autobook_price
}
acceptable_risk if {
  risk := input.resource.properties.airline_risk_score
  risk <= input.resource.properties.owner.autobook_risklevel
}
sufficient_advance if {
  departure_date_str := input.resource.properties.departure_date
  departure_date := time.parse_rfc3339_ns(departure_date_str)
  now := time.now_ns()
  delta_days := (departure_date - now) / 1000000000 / 60 / 60 / 24
  delta_days >= input.resource.properties.owner.autobook_leadtime
}

What this does:

  • Ensures booking cost doesn't exceed user's configured limit
  • Cost limit is per-user preference
  • Checks airline risk score against user's tolerance
  • Allows execution if risk score is missing (optional field)
  • Denies if risk exceeds configured threshold
  • Requires minimum advance notice before departure
  • Prevents last-minute autonomous bookings

Authz-API role:

  • Extracts planned_price from workflow item resource
  • Fetches autobook_price from user profile
  • Extracts airline_risk_score from workflow item resource, if present
  • Fetches autobook_risklevel from user profile
  • Extracts departure_date from workflow item resource
  • Fetches autobook_leadtime from user profile
  • Converts and normalizes prices, risk scores and times

docs.title: Step 8: Deny Reason Codes
docs.text:
The policy provides a structured reason code for every denial scenario. Reason codes follow the gate evaluation order:

# 1. Principal spoofing (no delegation)
reasons[code] if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  principal_id := input.subject.id
  owner_id := input.resource.properties.owner.id
  principal_id != owner_id
  not input.context.delegation.valid
  code := "auto_book.principal_spoofing"
}

# 2. Insufficient delegation permissions
reasons[code] if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  principal_id := input.subject.id
  owner_id := input.resource.properties.owner.id
  principal_id != owner_id
  input.context.delegation.valid == true
  not input.action.name in input.context.delegation.delegated_actions
  code := "auto_book.insufficient_delegation_permissions"
}

# 3. Persona mismatch
reasons[code] if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  code := "auto_book.persona_mismatch"
  authorized_principal
  not persona_valid
}

# 4. Persona inactive/suspended
reasons[code] if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  code := "auto_book.persona_inactive"
  authorized_principal
  persona_valid
  not owner_persona_active
}

# 5. Persona expired or not yet active
reasons[code] if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  code := "auto_book.persona_expired"
  authorized_principal
  persona_valid
  owner_persona_active
  not owner_persona_valid_time
}

# 6. No autobook consent
reasons[code] if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  code := "auto_book.no_consent"
  authorized_principal
  persona_valid
  owner_persona_active
  owner_persona_valid_time
  not has_consent
}

# 7. Airline risk too high
reasons[code] if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  code := "auto_book.airline_risk_too_high"
  authorized_principal
  has_consent
  not acceptable_risk
}

# 8. Cost limit exceeded
reasons[code] if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  code := "auto_book.cost_limit_exceeded"
  authorized_principal
  has_consent
  acceptable_risk
  not within_cost_limit
}

# 9. Insufficient advance notice
reasons[code] if {
  input.action.name == "execute"
  code := "auto_book.insufficient_advance_notice"
  authorized_principal
  has_consent
  acceptable_risk
  within_cost_limit
  not sufficient_advance
}

Reason Code Categories:

Code Description Gate auto_book.principal_spoofing No valid delegation exists Anti-spoofing auto_book.insufficient_delegation_permissions Delegation lacks execute action Anti-spoofing auto_book.persona_mismatch Subject persona doesn't match requirements Persona validation auto_book.persona_inactive Owner's persona status is not active Persona status auto_book.persona_expired Current time outside persona validity range Persona temporal auto_book.no_consent Autobook consent is false Consent auto_book.airline_risk_too_high Risk score exceeds threshold Risk gate auto_book.cost_limit_exceeded Price exceeds configured limit Cost gate auto_book.insufficient_advance_notice Departure too soon Lead time gate

What this does:

  • Provides structured reason codes for denials
  • Each failed gate has a corresponding reason
  • Reasons follow gate evaluation order (fail-fast)
  • Supports debugging and audit trails
  • Enables client-side error messages

Authz-API role:

  • Collects reasons array from OPA response
  • Returns reason codes to PEP for logging/debugging
  • Maps codes to human-readable messages

docs.title: Pattern 1: Fail-Closed
docs.text:
default allow := false

allow if {
  # Explicit conditions
}

Always deny unless explicitly allowed.

docs.title: Pattern 2: Multiple Allow Rules
docs.text:
allow if { condition_a }
allow if { condition_b }

Any rule can grant access (OR logic).

docs.title: Pattern 3: Gated Allow
docs.text:
allow if {
  gate_1
  gate_2
  gate_3
}

All gates must pass (AND logic).

docs.title: Pattern 4: Reason Tracking
docs.text:
reasons[code] if {
  not some_condition
  code := "policy.failure_reason"
}

Provide structured denial reasons.

docs.title: For Policy Authors
docs.text:
1. Fail-closed - Default to deny
  2. Explicit rules - Make allow conditions clear
  3. Reason codes - Provide debugging information
  4. Type safety - Ensure data is normalized before

docs.title: For Authz-API Developers
docs.text:
1. Normalize data - Convert types before OPA
  2. Validate inputs - Ensure required fields exist
  3. Enrich completely - Provide all data OPA needs
  4. Handle errors - Graceful degradation on PIP failures
  5. Log decisions - Audit all authorization outcomes

docs.title: For PEP Developers
docs.text:
1. Minimal requests - Send only intent, not policy data
  2. Consistent format - Use AuthZEN structure
  3. Include context - Provide principal when subject ≠ owner
  4. Enforce decisions - Always respect authz-api response

docs.title: Local Development
docs.text:
Policies are mounted from filesystem with hot-reload:

# docker-compose.yml
opa:
  volumes:
    - ./infra/opa/policies:/policies

docs.title: Production (GCP Cloud Run)
docs.text:
Policies are baked into the OPA container image:

# infra/opa/Dockerfile
COPY infra/opa/policies /policies

Having them baked in enables a strong version control based on a git repo. And with strong version control comes the ability for strong governance. The latter, hwoever, is a matter of process and organization, not a technical matter.

To update policies in production:

  1. Edit infra/opa/policies/auto_book.rego
  2. Rebuild OPA container
  3. Push to Artifact Registry
  4. Deploy to Cloud Run
docker build -f infra/opa/Dockerfile -t us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/PROJECT/flowpilot/opa:latest .
docker push us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/PROJECT/flowpilot/opa:latest
gcloud run deploy flowpilot-opa --image us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/PROJECT/flowpilot/opa:latest

docs.title: Integration Guide
docs.text: Add FlowPilot authorization to your web or mobile application.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
FlowPilot is a managed authorization service for demo purposes. It illustrates:

  • Multi-persona authorization (user as traveler vs. agent vs. admin)
  • Delegation management (user delegates to AI agent or another user)
  • Policy evaluation (consent checks, risk thresholds, custom rules)
  • Audit trails and explainable decisions

docs.title: Service Endpoints
docs.text:
Production API Base URL: https://flowpilot-domain-services-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app

All APIs require bearer access tokens.

docs.title: Available APIs
docs.text: API Purpose Endpoint Domain Services Manage workflows and workflow items /v1/workflows AuthZ Authorization decisions /v1/evaluate Delegation Manage delegations /v1/delegations User Profile User personas and preferences /v1/profile AI Agent Execute workflows via agent /v1/workflow-runs

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Firebase Project - Sign up at firebase.google.com
  2. FlowPilot Account - Contact us to get your project configured
  3. User Profile Storage - Set up Firestore to store user personas and preferences

docs.title: Step 1: Authenticate Your Users
docs.text:
FlowPilot uses Firebase Authentication for user identity. Integrate Firebase Auth in your app:

Web (JavaScript)iOS (Swift)Android (Kotlin) 
import { getAuth, signInWithEmailAndPassword } from 'firebase/auth';

const auth = getAuth();
const userCredential = await signInWithEmailAndPassword(
  auth, 
  email, 
  password
);

// Get ID token to pass to FlowPilot
const idToken = await userCredential.user.getIdToken();

import FirebaseAuth

Auth.auth().signIn(withEmail: email, password: password) { result, error in
    guard let user = result?.user else { return }

    user.getIDToken { token, error in
        // Use token with FlowPilot APIs
        let idToken = token
    }
}

import com.google.firebase.auth.FirebaseAuth

val auth = FirebaseAuth.getInstance()
auth.signInWithEmailAndPassword(email, password)
    .addOnCompleteListener { task ->
        if (task.isSuccessful) {
            val user = auth.currentUser
            user?.getIdToken(true)?.addOnCompleteListener { tokenTask ->
                val idToken = tokenTask.result?.token
                // Use token with FlowPilot APIs
            }
        }
    }

docs.title: Step 2: Manage User Profiles
docs.text: FlowPilot provides a User Profile API to manage user personas and preferences. No Firebase SDK required.

docs.title: Get User Profile
docs.text:
const response = await fetch(
  'https://flowpilot-user-profile-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app/v1/profile',
  {
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${idToken}`
    }
  }
);

const profile = await response.json();
console.log('User persona:', profile.personas);

docs.title: Update User Profile
docs.text:
const response = await fetch(
  'https://flowpilot-user-profile-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app/v1/profile',
  {
    method: 'PUT',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${idToken}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      personas: ["traveler"],
      autobook_consent: true,
      autobook_price: 500,
      autobook_leadtime: 30,
      autobook_risklevel: 50
    })
  }
);

const updatedProfile = await response.json();

docs.title: Find Users by Persona
docs.text:
Find delegation candidates (e.g., travel agents):

const response = await fetch(
  'https://flowpilot-user-profile-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app/v1/users?persona=travel-agent',
  {
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${idToken}`
    }
  }
);

const { users } = await response.json();
console.log('Available agents:', users);

docs.title: Profile Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description sub string User UUID (from Firebase Auth) personas string[] List of personas: ["traveler", "travel-agent", "admin"] autobook_consent boolean Consent for AI agent execution autobook_price number Max price agent can book autobook_leadtime number Max days in advance autobook_risklevel number Risk tolerance (0-100)

docs.title: Step 3: Create Workflows
docs.text:
Call the Domain Services API to create a workflow (e.g., a travel booking):

const response = await fetch(
  'https://flowpilot-domain-services-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app/v1/workflows',
  {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${idToken}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      template_id: 'trip-to-milan',
      principal_sub: user.uid,
      start_date: '2026-02-01',
      persona: 'traveler'
    })
  }
);

const workflow = await response.json();
console.log('Created workflow:', workflow.workflow_id);

docs.title: Step 4: Delegate to AI Agent
docs.text:
Allow the AI agent to execute workflows on the user's behalf:

const delegationResponse = await fetch(
  'https://flowpilot-delegation-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app/v1/delegations',
  {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${idToken}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      principal_id: user.uid,
      delegate_id: 'agent-runner',  // FlowPilot's AI agent ID
      workflow_id: workflow.workflow_id,
      scope: ['execute'],
      expires_in_days: 7
    })
  }
);

docs.title: Step 5: Execute Workflow via Agent
docs.text:
Trigger the AI agent to execute the workflow:

const runResponse = await fetch(
  'https://flowpilot-ai-agent-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app/v1/workflow-runs',
  {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${idToken}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      workflow_id: workflow.workflow_id,
      principal_sub: user.uid,
      dry_run: false,
      persona: 'traveler'
    })
  }
);

const runResult = await runResponse.json();

// Check results
runResult.results.forEach(item => {
  console.log(`Item ${item.workflow_item_id}: ${item.decision}`);
  if (item.decision === 'deny') {
    console.log('Reason:', item.reason_codes);
  }
});

docs.title: Step 6: Handle Authorization Decisions
docs.text:
The agent returns structured authorization decisions:

{
  "decision": "deny",
  "reason_codes": ["rego.deny.consent_required"],
  "advice": [
    {
      "type": "deny",
      "message": "User consent not provided for auto-booking"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Common Reason Codes
docs.text: Reason Code Meaning rego.deny.consent_required User hasn't consented to auto-booking rego.deny.price_exceeded Item price exceeds user's threshold rego.deny.leadtime_exceeded Booking too far in advance rego.deny.risk_exceeded Risk level too high for user delegation.not_found No delegation exists delegation.expired Delegation has expired

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
Here's a complete workflow in JavaScript:

import { initializeApp } from 'firebase/app';
import { getAuth, signInWithEmailAndPassword } from 'firebase/auth';

// 1. Initialize Firebase
const app = initializeApp(firebaseConfig);
const auth = getAuth(app);

// 2. Sign in user
const userCredential = await signInWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
const idToken = await userCredential.user.getIdToken();

// 3. Create workflow
const workflowResponse = await fetch(
  'https://flowpilot-domain-services-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app/v1/workflows',
  {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${idToken}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      template_id: 'trip-to-milan',
      principal_sub: userCredential.user.uid,
      start_date: '2026-02-01',
      persona: 'traveler'
    })
  }
);
const workflow = await workflowResponse.json();

// 4. Delegate to agent
await fetch(
  'https://flowpilot-delegation-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app/v1/delegations',
  {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${idToken}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      principal_id: userCredential.user.uid,
      delegate_id: 'agent-runner',
      workflow_id: workflow.workflow_id,
      expires_in_days: 7
    })
  }
);

// 5. Execute via agent
const runResponse = await fetch(
  'https://flowpilot-ai-agent-api-737191827545.us-central1.run.app/v1/workflow-runs',
  {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${idToken}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      workflow_id: workflow.workflow_id,
      principal_sub: userCredential.user.uid,
      dry_run: false
    })
  }
);
const result = await runResponse.json();

console.log('Execution results:', result);

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get FlowPilot running locally in 5 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker and Docker Compose
  • make (optional, for convenience commands)
  • curl or similar HTTP client for testing

docs.title: Step 1: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/flowpilot.git
cd flowpilot

docs.title: Step 2: Configure Environment
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the root directory:

cat > .env << EOF
KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_USERNAME=admin
KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin123
KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET=your-secret-here
AGENT_CLIENT_SECRET=your-agent-secret-here
EOF

Security Note

Never commit the .env file to version control. It contains sensitive credentials.

docs.title: Step 3: Start the Stack
docs.text:
The easiest way to start FlowPilot is using the provided script:

make up
# Or directly:
./bin/stack-up.sh

This script will:

  1. Build and start all Docker containers
  2. Wait for Keycloak to be ready (can take 60+ seconds)
  3. Provision test users and agent relationships
  4. Configure OIDC clients

docs.title: Step 4: Verify Services Are Running
docs.text:
Check the status of all services:

make status
# Or:
docker compose ps

You should see all services in "Up" status:

  • keycloak
  • opa
  • flowpilot-authz-api
  • flowpilot-delegation-api
  • flowpilot-domain-services-api
  • flowpilot-ai-agent-api

docs.title: Step 5: Run Integration Tests
docs.text:
Verify everything works by running the test suite:

python3 tests/user_based_testing.py

Expected output: 

Test Results:
- Kathleen: Allowed=0, Denied=3, Errors=0 ✓
- Peter: Allowed=0, Denied=3, Errors=0 ✓
- Carlo: Allowed=3, Denied=0, Errors=0 ✓

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text:
curl http://localhost:8002/health  # authz-api
curl http://localhost:8003/health  # domain-services-api
curl http://localhost:8004/health  # ai-agent-api
curl http://localhost:8005/health  # delegation-api

docs.title: Get an Access Token
docs.text:
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST \
  "http://localhost:8080/realms/flowpilot/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials" \
  -d "client_id=flowpilot-agent" \
  -d "client_secret=$AGENT_CLIENT_SECRET" | jq -r '.access_token')

echo $TOKEN

docs.title: Create a Workflow
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8003/v1/workflows \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "template_id": "trip-to-milan",
    "principal_sub": "user-uuid-here",
    "start_date": "2026-02-01",
    "persona": "traveler"
  }'

docs.title: View Logs
docs.text:
# All services
make logs

# Specific service
docker compose logs -f flowpilot-authz-api
docker compose logs -f opa

docs.title: Stop the Stack
docs.text:
make down
# Or:
./bin/stack-down.sh

docs.title: Reset Everything
docs.text:
make reset
# Or:
./bin/stack-reset.sh

This wipes all volumes and gives you a clean slate.

docs.title: Keycloak Not Ready
docs.text:
If provisioning fails, wait longer:

docker compose logs -f keycloak
# Wait for "Listening on: https://0.0.0.0:8443"

docs.title: Port Conflicts
docs.text:
Ensure ports 8080, 8181, 8443, and 8002-8005 are available:

lsof -i :8080
lsof -i :8002

docs.title: Services Won't Start
docs.text:
Try a full rebuild:

docker compose down -v
docker compose up -d --build

For more troubleshooting tips, see the Troubleshooting Guide.
